title: The Upper Room
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A ghost story novel. A family moves into a pre-war apartment building in Boston only to discover the upstairs neighbor died in 1952 and never left.
long_description:
Sarah Vasquez and her twelve-year-old son Leo move into a pre-war apartment building in Boston's Back Bay, hoping for a fresh start after a difficult divorce. The rent is impossibly low, and the reason emerges slowly: no one stays long in 4B. The elderly woman who lived upstairs died in 1952 and never left. She walks the hallway at night, plays records on a Victrola no one owns, and she has been waiting a long time for someone to notice her.

As Sarah and Leo settle in, the presence in the upper room begins to make itself known — not with malice, but with an aching, lonely persistence. Through old letters, a forgotten photograph, and patient observation, they uncover the story of Eleanor Pendleton, a woman who died alone with an unsent letter to a man named Thomas, a soldier lost in the Korean War. The ghost has been waiting fifty-four years for someone to read her words aloud so she can finally let go.

The Upper Room is a ghost story about loneliness, courage, and the things we leave unsaid. It asks what it means to be seen, remembered, and finally released — and whether love can survive even the silence of the grave.
